result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would eliminate the capacity for ideological certainty—the kind that shuts down curiosity, refuses doubt, and silences others in the name of absolute truth. Not to erase belief or conviction, but to make every person carry a quiet awareness that they might be wrong, and that others might hold pieces of the same truth from different angles.

That small crack in certainty could open space for humility, patience, and genuine listening. It wouldn't solve every problem, but I think it would make so many others—war, hatred, exploitation, environmental neglect—just a little harder to justify, and a little easier to heal.
